file_insights.changes_summary: Added complete render loop implementation with three-zone layout and ratatui Terminal integration
key_additions: Imported ratatui backend (CrosstermBackend), layout (Constraint, Layout), widgets (Block, Paragraph), and Terminal
Created Terminal instance after TerminalGuard initialization for proper resource lifecycle
Implemented draw() function with three-zone vertical layout: header (2 rows fixed), body (Min(0) flexible), footer (1 row fixed)
Added initial draw() call before event loop to render starting state
Added draw() call after event processing for event-driven UI updates
file_insights.code_quality: Follows k9s-style three-zone pattern; uses ratatui's Layout::vertical with explicit constraints; placeholder widgets ready for future feature integration

patterns_discovered.description: TerminalGuard wraps terminal setup; Drop impl ensures alternate screen + raw mode restoration regardless of error path
patterns_discovered.evidence: TerminalGuard::new() called before Terminal creation; _guard kept in scope through entire run_ui()
patterns_discovered.applicability: Essential for TUI cleanup guarantees; prevents terminal corruption on panic or early exit

patterns_discovered.description: Layout::vertical with fixed-size header/footer (Constraint::Length) and flexible body (Constraint::Min(0)) divides terminal into predictable regions
patterns_discovered.evidence: Layout::vertical([Length(2), Min(0), Length(1)]).split(area) creates three zones; header=2 rows, body=remaining, footer=1 row
patterns_discovered.applicability: Standard for dashboard/kanban UIs; k9s reference confirmed in spec 029

patterns_discovered.description: UI redraw triggered after each event (keyboard, timer, file watcher) rather than continuous polling
patterns_discovered.evidence: draw(&mut terminal, &app)? called in event loop after state updates; initial render before loop starts
patterns_discovered.applicability: Efficient for event-based systems; reduces CPU usage; matches crossterm async event model

patterns_discovered.description: Temporary widgets with hardcoded text and styling; designed for incremental feature implementation without blocking core architecture
patterns_discovered.evidence: Header, body, footer paragraphs use placeholder text ('bop · --:--:-- · placeholder header', 'kanban body'); styled with Color enum
patterns_discovered.applicability: Unblocks subtask completion; allows downstream tasks (3-1 header, 2-1 kanban, 3-2 footer) to replace independently

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Terminal creation must follow TerminalGuard initialization
gotchas_discovered.explanation: TerminalGuard enters alternate screen + raw mode via crossterm. Terminal backend must be created AFTER to avoid state conflicts. Reversed order causes restore() to drop before Terminal is built.

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Initial draw() call required before event loop
gotchas_discovered.explanation: Without initial render, screen shows blank until first event. Users perceive lag or unresponsiveness. Initial draw() establishes visual feedback immediately.
gotchas_discovered.code_location: run_ui(): draw(&mut terminal, &app)? line 35, before loop

gotchas_discovered.explanation: Layout::vertical([Length(2), Min(0), Length(1)]) must have flexible zone (Min(0)) in middle. Swapping with fixed zones causes incorrect space allocation or panic on small terminals.

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Paragraph widgets without block look unstyled
gotchas_discovered.explanation: Footer paragraph has no border/block; body has Block::bordered() for visual distinction. Intentional for now, but testing revealed footer blends with body on some terminal themes.

core_achievements: Render loop fully operational; enters alternate screen on startup, restores on exit
Three-zone layout correctly divides terminal; header/footer fixed size, body fills remaining space
Event-driven redraw architecture in place; app state updates trigger terminal.draw()
Placeholder widgets render without errors; ready for feature-specific implementations
approach_outcome.test_coverage: Manual verification: bop ui enters alternate screen, displays three zones with placeholder text, quits cleanly on 'q' or Ctrl-C, restores terminal state
unblocked_downstream: Subtask 3-1 (header widgets: clock, mode indicator, sidebar) can implement header zone independently
Subtask 2-1 (kanban columns and card rendering) can implement body zone independently
Subtask 3-2 (footer keybinding legend) can implement footer zone independently

recommendations.recommendation: Extract layout constraints to named constants
recommendations.rationale: Magic numbers [2, 0, 1] are meaningful in context but repeated. Constants like HEADER_HEIGHT=2, FOOTER_HEIGHT=1 improve readability and ease future terminal dimension changes.
recommendations.example: const HEADER_HEIGHT: u16 = 2; const FOOTER_HEIGHT: u16 = 1; Layout::vertical([Constraint::Length(HEADER_HEIGHT), Constraint::Min(0), Constraint::Length(FOOTER_HEIGHT)])

recommendations.recommendation: Move draw() function to separate theme/render module as complexity grows
recommendations.rationale: draw() will expand significantly as widgets replace placeholders. Separating into ui/render.rs or ui/theme.rs keeps mod.rs focused on run_ui() orchestration.
recommendations.example: Create ui/render.rs with pub fn render_layout(frame, app) and theme structs

recommendations.recommendation: Add integration test verifying three-zone layout constraints under various terminal sizes
recommendations.rationale: Small terminals (< 4 rows) may violate Min(0) contract. Test with edge cases: 3 rows, 5 rows, 200x50.
recommendations.example: Test in crates/bop-cli/tests/ using fake Terminal backend or snapshot rendering

recommendations.recommendation: Monitor draw() frequency and consider debouncing rapid events if CPU spike observed
recommendations.rationale: Current design redraws on EVERY event including tick (~100ms). If many fs watcher events queue, redraw may stall. Implement frame-rate cap (e.g., 30 FPS) if needed.
recommendations.example: Add Instant timestamp in draw(), skip if < 33ms elapsed
